title: Peeking Inside the Black-Box: A Survey on Explainable Artificial Intelligence
abstract_or_note: Survey note describing explainable AI as a response to black box machine learning and the need for transparency, interpretability, and trust.
relevance_note: Useful for defining XAI and its conceptual foundations.

title: Interpretable Machine Learning
abstract_or_note: Introduces interpretable machine learning methods including feature importance, partial dependence, surrogate models, and model-specific interpretation.
relevance_note: Supports discussion of interpretable models and post-hoc methods.

title: Why Should I Trust You? Explaining the Predictions of Any Classifier
abstract_or_note: Presents LIME as a post-hoc local surrogate explanation method intended to help users understand individual model predictions.
relevance_note: Relevant for methods, opportunities, and user trust.

title: A Unified Approach to Interpreting Model Predictions
abstract_or_note: Introduces SHAP as a feature attribution method connecting model explanations with game-theoretic reasoning.
relevance_note: Relevant for XAI methods and practical interpretation of model outputs.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence: Concepts, Taxonomies, Opportunities and Challenges
abstract_or_note: Discusses XAI concepts, taxonomies, opportunities, and challenges in intelligent systems, including transparency and trustworthiness.
relevance_note: Useful for broad thesis framing across definitions, opportunities, and limitations.

title: Towards a Rigorous Science of Interpretable Machine Learning
abstract_or_note: Argues for clearer evaluation practices for interpretability and distinguishes different evaluation settings and user needs.

title: Explanation in Artificial Intelligence: Insights from the Social Sciences
abstract_or_note: Highlights that explanations should consider human reasoning, contrastive explanation, and stakeholder understanding.
relevance_note: Relevant for human-centered XAI and communication.

title: Explainability for Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare
abstract_or_note: Discusses healthcare AI, clinicians, patient safety, accountability, and the limits of explanation in clinical decision support.
relevance_note: Supports practical implications in high-risk healthcare contexts.

title: European Union Regulations on Algorithmic Decision-Making and a Right to Explanation
abstract_or_note: Connects transparency, accountability, governance, and legal debates around automated decision-making.
relevance_note: Relevant for governance, accountability, and trustworthy AI.

title: Stop Explaining Black Box Machine Learning Models for High Stakes Decisions
abstract_or_note: Critiques reliance on post-hoc explanations in high-stakes settings and argues for interpretable models where possible.
relevance_note: Useful for limitations, ethical risk, and practical implications.

title: Explainable Machine Learning in Deployment
abstract_or_note: Discusses deployment challenges, stakeholder needs, explanation monitoring, and operational issues in real-world AI systems.
relevance_note: Relevant for applied XAI and real-world implementation.

title: DARPA's Explainable Artificial Intelligence Program
abstract_or_note: Describes the goals of XAI research, including interpretable models, user understanding, and explanation effectiveness.
relevance_note: Useful for foundations, opportunities, and human-centered evaluation.

title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework
abstract_or_note: Provides governance-oriented guidance related to trustworthy AI, risk management, accountability, transparency, and responsible use.
relevance_note: Supports governance and trustworthy AI discussion.

title: Explainable AI in Credit Risk Management
abstract_or_note: Discusses finance AI, credit risk, model transparency, accountability, and practical implications for regulated decision-making.
relevance_note: Relevant for finance, governance, and applied XAI.

title: Explainable AI for Cybersecurity
abstract_or_note: Reviews cybersecurity AI uses where explanations can support analyst understanding, trust, and investigation of model alerts.
relevance_note: Relevant for practical implications and human-centered security work.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Autonomous Driving
abstract_or_note: Reviews XAI for autonomous systems, including safety, human trust, decision explanation, and evaluation challenges.
relevance_note: Relevant for practical implications, trust, and limitations.
